seoTitle: How to Copy a Power BI Visual Into PowerPoint
metaDescription: Paste pixel-perfect Power BI charts into PowerPoint using the Copy as Image with Caption feature, available in both Desktop and the cloud service.
intro: Blurry screenshots and manual snipping tools produce low-resolution graphics that distort during presentations. Power BI's Copy as Image with Caption function exports pixel-perfect visuals directly into your slide deck, preserving embedded filter states and metadata links. This guide covers exporting from both Desktop and the cloud service.

steps.heading: Select the visual in Power BI Desktop
steps.body: Click the specific chart or table you want to export and open its context menu header options.

steps.heading: Use Copy as Image with Caption
steps.body: Choose the Copy as Image with Caption function to capture the visual at high resolution along with its active filter state.

steps.heading: Paste into PowerPoint
steps.body: Switch to your PowerPoint slide and paste the copied visual, which inserts as a high-fidelity smart object rather than a blurry screenshot.

steps.heading: Copy from the cloud service if needed
steps.body: If working from a browser, trigger the same automated copy menu from the Power BI Service to export visuals without opening Desktop.

steps.heading: Manage hyperlink references
steps.body: Check whether the pasted object retains a direct hyperlink reference back to the live report if you want viewers to click through for more detail.

faq.question: Is Copy as Image with Caption better than a screenshot?
faq.answer: Yes, it captures the visual as a high-resolution vector-based object rather than a flat, low-quality screenshot.

faq.question: Does the copied visual stay linked to live data?
faq.answer: The pasted image itself is static, though it can retain a hyperlink reference back to the live report for further exploration.

faq.question: Can I copy visuals from the browser version of Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, the Power BI Service in the browser includes the same automated copy function found in Desktop.

faq.question: Will the copied visual include active filters?
faq.answer: Yes, the caption feature captures the visual's current filter state at the time of copying.
